findings: Independent verification confirms the title, scientific name, family, measurements, behavior, habitat, and listed field marks. Cornell gives the exact displayed measurement ranges; Audubon gives closely matching rounded ranges.

The central bird is a plausible adult male Red-winged Blackbird: glossy black plumage, stocky broad-shouldered form, pointed conical bill, dark eye, medium squared-to-rounded tail, dark legs/feet, and a red epaulet with yellow lower border all agree with the supplied field references and verified descriptions.

No material text or species-identification error found. The bill and feet are somewhat stylized, and only the near wing is visible in the side-on central pose, but the anatomy is coherent: one head, one beak, two legs, one tail, and an implied far wing behind the body.
No place name, ZIP code, coordinates, map, or local-observation detail appears on the plate.

verification_sources.title: Cornell Lab of Ornithology — Red-winged Blackbird Identification
